title: Dog Age by Breed Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Dog Age in Human Years by Breed
description: Convert your dog's age to human years by breed and size using the 2020 AVMA logarithmic formula. Accurate, free, instant results.
intro: The **1 dog year = 7 human years** rule is a myth. Modern science (AVMA 2020 formula, based on DNA methylation) shows dogs age much faster in the first 2 years (~31 human years) then slow down. Plus, **breed size matters**: large dogs age faster than small ones. This calculator uses the 2020 AVMA logarithmic formula adjusted by breed size.
keyTakeaway: **AVMA 2020 Formula**: human age = **16 × ln(dog age) + 31**. Toy/small breeds: −10%. Large breeds: +15%. First year = 15 human years. Second year = 24 human years.
useCases: Understand your dog's true life stage and development.
Share your dog's age in human years on social media.
Decide if it's time for senior or geriatric care.
Compare aging between two different breed sizes.
Plan activities and exercise based on their actual age.

dataUpdate.notes: Veterinary data and breed standards. Stable table.

example.title: Example: 5-Year-Old Beagle
example.result: A 5-year-old Beagle equals approximately **57 human years**. Mature adult.

explanation:
## AVMA 2020 Formula (Modern Standard)

```
human_age = 16 × ln(dog_age) + 31
```

Based on a DNA methylation study of 104 Labrador Retrievers published in Cell Systems (2020). This is the most biologically accurate formula available.

## Quick Reference Table

| Dog Age (years) | Human Age (medium breed) |
|---|---|
| 1 | 15 |
| 2 | 24 |
| 3 | 29 |
| 4 | 33 |
| 5 | 37 |
| 6 | 42 |
| 7 | 47 |
| 8 | 51 |
| 10 | 56 |
| 12 | 64 |
| 15 | 71 |

## Adjustment by Breed Size

Small dogs live longer and age more slowly in adulthood. Large dogs do the opposite.

- **Toy/Small**: factor 0.9
- **Medium**: factor 1.0
- **Large**: factor 1.10
- **Giant**: factor 1.20

## Life Stages

| Human Age | Stage |
|---|---|
| <20 | Puppy/Juvenile |
| 20–40 | Young Adult |
| 40–60 | Mature Adult |
| 60–75 | Senior |
| >75 | Geriatric |

## Related Calculators

- [Dog Lifespan by Breed](/dog-lifespan-by-breed)

faq.q: Is the 7-year rule for dog age actually true?
faq.a: No, it's a myth. The modern AVMA 2020 formula shows the first year equals 15 human years, the second year equals 24 human years, then growth slows. It's logarithmic, not linear.

faq.q: How do I convert my dog's age to human years?
faq.a: Use the formula: human age = 16 × ln(dog age) + 31, then adjust by breed size: small breeds get −10%, large breeds get +15%.

faq.q: Do large breed dogs age faster than small breeds?
faq.a: Yes. A 7-year-old Great Dane is roughly 55 in human years, while a 7-year-old Chihuahua is roughly 45. Larger dogs experience greater cellular stress and shorter lifespans.

faq.q: At what age is a dog considered senior?
faq.a: Large breeds: 6–7 years old. Medium breeds: 8 years old. Small breeds: 9–10 years old. All roughly equal 60+ human years.

faq.q: Why do bigger dogs have shorter lifespans?
faq.a: Large dogs experience higher cellular stress from their larger body mass, faster cell division, and greater cancer risk. It's fundamental biology.

faq.q: Does spaying or neutering affect the age calculation?
faq.a: The formula itself doesn't change, but spaying/neutering increases your dog's lifespan by reducing the risk of hormonal cancers.

faq.q: What's the difference between 'mature adult' and 'senior' for dogs?
faq.a: Mature adult (40–60 human years / 4–7 dog years): slower metabolism, needs adjusted exercise. Senior (60+ human years): regular vet visits, joint support, preventive care.

sources.name: Cell Systems - Quantitative translation of dog-to-human aging
